title: Budgeting
description: Test your understanding of budgeting, operating budgets, financial budgets, capital budgets, master budgets, static budgets and flexible budgets.

questions.question: What is a budget?
options: A financial plan for a future period
A report of historical transactions only
A calculation of the market value of a company
A list of all unpaid invoices
questions.explanation: A budget is a financial plan that estimates future revenues, costs, cash flows and resource needs.

questions.question: What is one of the main purposes of budgeting?
options: To eliminate all uncertainty
To help managers plan and allocate resources
To replace financial accounting
To guarantee future profits
questions.explanation: Budgets help managers plan future activities and allocate limited resources.

questions.question: Which budget usually serves as the starting point for the budgeting process?
options: Sales budget
Cash budget
Capital budget
Administrative expense budget
questions.explanation: Expected sales normally determine production, purchasing, staffing and cash requirements.

questions.question: Which budget focuses primarily on day-to-day business activities?
options: Operating budget
Capital budget
Financial budget
Investment portfolio budget
questions.explanation: The operating budget covers activities such as sales, production, materials, labor and operating expenses.

questions.question: Which item would normally be included in a financial budget?
options: Cash budget
Direct labor budget
Production budget
Sales commission budget
questions.explanation: Financial budgets include cash planning, financing needs and the budgeted financial position.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a capital budget?
options: To plan long-term investments in assets
To calculate daily sales revenue
To record historical depreciation
To allocate overhead between products
questions.explanation: Capital budgets evaluate major long-term investments such as machinery, buildings and technology.

questions.question: What is a master budget?
options: A combined plan containing the organization's individual budgets
A budget prepared only by the accounting department
A plan covering only fixed costs
A budget that cannot be revised
questions.explanation: The master budget combines the operating and financial budgets into one comprehensive plan.

questions.question: A company expects to sell 40,000 units and wants ending inventory of 5,000 units. Beginning inventory is 3,000 units. How many units should it produce?
options: 38,000 units
40,000 units
42,000 units
48,000 units
questions.explanation: Required production equals expected sales plus desired ending inventory minus beginning inventory: 40,000 + 5,000 − 3,000 = 42,000 units.

questions.question: Why is a cash budget important even for a profitable company?
options: A profitable company may still lack enough cash to pay its obligations
Profit and cash are always identical
Cash budgets eliminate the need for financing
Profitable companies do not need liquidity planning
questions.explanation: Revenue and expenses may be recognized at different times from cash receipts and payments, creating liquidity problems.

questions.question: A company prepares a budget based on sales of 60,000 units. Actual sales are 75,000 units. What is the main weakness of comparing actual costs directly with the original budget?
options: The activity levels are different
The original budget includes no fixed costs
Actual costs cannot be measured
Sales volume has no effect on costs
questions.explanation: A direct comparison may be misleading because variable costs are expected to change when activity differs from the original budget.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of a flexible budget compared with a static budget?
options: It adjusts for the actual level of activity
It eliminates all forecasting errors
It only includes fixed costs
It never needs to be updated
questions.explanation: A flexible budget adjusts expected revenues and costs based on the actual level of activity, making performance evaluations more meaningful.

questions.question: A company prepared a static budget for producing 10,000 units but actually produced 12,000 units. Which budget should be used to evaluate performance?
options: A flexible budget based on 12,000 units
The original static budget
The previous year's budget
Only the cash budget
questions.explanation: A flexible budget reflects the actual activity level and provides a fair comparison with actual results.

questions.question: Which budget helps management determine whether the company will have enough cash to pay suppliers and employees?
options: Cash budget
Production budget
Sales budget
Direct labor budget
questions.explanation: The cash budget forecasts cash inflows and outflows to help managers maintain sufficient liquidity.

questions.question: Why is coordination between departments important during the budgeting process?
options: Because one department's plans often affect the resource needs of other departments
Because departments prepare completely independent budgets
Because only the finance department uses budgets
Because coordination reduces depreciation expense
questions.explanation: Sales forecasts influence production, purchasing, staffing and financing, requiring departments to work together.

questions.question: A company expects sales to increase significantly next year. Which budget will most likely need to be revised first?
options: Production budget
Historical income statement
Previous year's balance sheet
Depreciation schedule
questions.explanation: Higher expected sales require adjustments to production so sufficient inventory can be produced.

questions.question: A manager compares actual costs with a static budget and concludes that costs are too high. However, production was 20% higher than expected. What is the main problem with this conclusion?
options: The comparison ignores the higher activity level
Static budgets include only fixed costs
Actual costs should never be compared with budgets
Production volume does not affect costs
questions.explanation: Higher production naturally increases many variable costs, so a flexible budget should be used instead.

questions.question: Why do organizations prepare a master budget?
options: To combine all individual budgets into one comprehensive financial plan
To eliminate the need for departmental budgets
To calculate depreciation expenses
To determine historical profits
questions.explanation: The master budget integrates all budgets and provides management with an overall view of expected financial performance.

questions.question: Which situation best demonstrates the value of budgeting?
options: Management identifies a future cash shortage early enough to arrange financing
Historical financial statements are prepared more quickly
Depreciation expenses are automatically reduced
Sales prices become fixed for the entire year
questions.explanation: Budgets allow managers to identify potential problems before they occur, giving them time to respond.

questions.question: Why should managers regularly update or revise budgets?
options: Business conditions, demand and costs often change during the year
Accounting standards require daily budget revisions
Budgets automatically become inaccurate after one month
Only revised budgets can be audited
questions.explanation: Budgets are based on assumptions, so they should be reviewed whenever significant business conditions change.

questions.question: Which statement best explains why budgeting is considered an important management tool?
options: It helps managers plan, coordinate resources and evaluate performance
It guarantees that all financial targets will be achieved
It completely removes business risk
It replaces managerial decision making
questions.explanation: Budgeting supports planning and decision making, but it cannot eliminate uncertainty or guarantee success.

questions.question: A company experiences a sudden increase in raw material prices halfway through the year. What is the most appropriate managerial response regarding the budget?
options: Review and revise the budget to reflect the new conditions
Ignore the increase because budgets cannot be changed
Continue evaluating managers using the original assumptions
Reduce all department budgets by the same percentage
questions.explanation: Budgets should support decision making. Significant changes in business conditions often require managers to revise their plans.

questions.question: A company consistently meets its budget targets by postponing necessary maintenance until the following year. Why is this a poor budgeting practice?
options: It improves short-term results while potentially creating larger future costs
Maintenance expenses are never included in budgets
Budgets should never include repair costs
Postponing maintenance always reduces total operating costs
questions.explanation: Budgets should encourage long-term value creation, not short-term decisions that increase future costs.

questions.question: A department manager intentionally underestimates expected sales so the department can easily exceed its budget targets. What is this behavior commonly called?
options: Budgetary slack
Flexible budgeting
Rolling forecasting
Incremental budgeting
questions.explanation: Budgetary slack occurs when managers intentionally make budgets easier to achieve by understating revenues or overstating costs.

questions.question: Which situation best demonstrates how budgeting supports strategic planning?
options: Management allocates additional resources to products expected to experience strong future demand
Management records historical accounting transactions
The accounting department calculates depreciation
Employees are paid according to last year's salaries
questions.explanation: Budgets help managers direct resources toward future opportunities that support the organization's strategy.

questions.question: A company's actual sales are significantly lower than expected. Which budget is most likely to require revision first to maintain realistic planning?
options: Production budget
Historical balance sheet
Depreciation schedule
Shareholder equity statement
questions.explanation: Lower sales reduce production requirements, which affects purchasing, labor and cash planning.

questions.question: A ship management company notices that one vessel's maintenance costs are consistently exceeding budget. What should management do first?
options: Investigate the reasons for the variance before changing the budget
Immediately reduce future maintenance spending
Ignore the variance because unexpected repairs always occur
Replace the vessel without further analysis
questions.explanation: Managers should understand the cause of budget deviations before deciding whether corrective action or budget revisions are necessary.

questions.question: A company prepares detailed annual budgets but never compares them with actual performance. What is the greatest weakness of this approach?
options: Management loses an important opportunity to learn from deviations and improve future decisions
Budgets automatically become inaccurate
Cash flow can no longer be measured
Financial statements cannot be prepared
questions.explanation: Budgets are most valuable when actual results are compared with planned performance to identify improvements.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between budgeting and managerial decision making?
options: Budgets provide information that supports decisions but should not replace managerial judgment
Budgets determine every business decision automatically
Managers should always follow the budget regardless of changing conditions
Budgets eliminate uncertainty from business planning
questions.explanation: Budgets are decision-support tools. Managers must still apply professional judgment when conditions change.

questions.question: Why might a flexible budget provide a more accurate performance evaluation than a static budget?
options: Because it adjusts expected revenues and costs to the actual activity level
Because it eliminates forecasting errors
Because it ignores variable costs
Because it focuses only on cash transactions
questions.explanation: Flexible budgets separate the effect of changing activity levels from actual operational performance.

questions.question: What is the most important purpose of budgeting in managerial accounting?
options: To support planning, coordination, resource allocation and continuous improvement throughout the organization
To guarantee that actual results exactly match expectations
To eliminate all financial risk
To ensure departments never exceed their spending limits under any circumstances
questions.explanation: Budgeting is primarily a planning and management tool that helps organizations achieve their strategic objectives while improving decision making and performance.
